settings.title: Settings
settings.layoutDescription: Complete this section to configure your company's operational data and customize your platform experience.

contacts.title: Client contacts
contacts.checkExtensionDescription:
This section is part of your credit policies. Here you define the conditions under which your company will allow extending the maturity of checks issued by debtors.
Extensions should be managed as a controlled exception, as they directly impact financial exposure and payment discipline of the portfolio. For this, you must define following parameters...
contacts.extensionRequestPeriodTooltip:
Number of days in advance the debtor must request the extension before the check matures.
This avoids reactive decisions and allows proper risk assessment.
contacts.annualExtensionsTooltip:
Sets how many extensions the same debtor can request within a year.
This parameter helps control recurrences and detect potential deterioration in payment behavior.
contacts.maxExtensionPeriodTooltip:
Maximum number of days a check can be extended.
Should be defined considering banking policies and internal implicit financing limits.
contacts.approversTooltip:
Users responsible for authorizing or rejecting requests.
This ensures traceability and internal control in exceptional decisions.
contacts.extensionCriteria: These criteria determine which requests will be accepted, which will require approval, and when they will be automatically blocked for policy non-compliance.

validation.selectContactType: Must select at least one contact type
validation.addAtLeastOneContact: Must add at least one contact
validation.keepAtLeastOneContact: Must keep at least one contact
validation.selectApprover: Must select at least one approver

debtors.segmentationDescription: Classify your clients into segments based on their billing level and payment behavior (DBT). The combination of Billing and DBT will allow Quironix to determine management priority, risk quadrants, and automatic task assignment. For it to work correctly, you must define the ranges that will determine each level:
debtors.billingTooltip: Identifies the financial weight of each client in your portfolio.
debtors.billingHigh: Clients with the highest billing volume.
debtors.dbtTooltip: Measures the average number of days a client takes to pay after the due date.
debtors.dbtHigh: Clients who pay well above the agreed term (e.g., more than 15 days late).
debtors.dbtMedium: Clients who pay with some delay (e.g., between 8 and 15 days).
debtors.dbtLow: Clients who pay on time or with little delay (less than 7 days).
debtors.dbtWarning: If ranges are not properly defined, portfolio prioritization may not reflect the actual financial reality of the business.
debtors.autoAdjustDescription: In this section you define the rules under which Quironix can automatically compensate or adjust differences between invoices and credit notes, without manual intervention. Proper configuration helps reduce operational tasks and keep accounting balances aligned with financial reality.
debtors.autoNettingTooltip: Allows automatic compensation between invoices and credit notes that are properly referenced to each other. If this option is disabled, compensations must be managed manually
debtors.amountFromTooltip:
Defines the minimum and maximum value a difference can have for the system to perform an automatic adjustment.
This range controls the tolerance level for small differences (e.g., roundings or minor adjustments).
debtors.defaultPoliciesDescription: In this section you define the rules that will allow Quironix to automatically determine when a payment commitment should be considered fulfilled or defaulted. These parameters are essential for measuring the reliability of your debtors and feeding credibility, prioritization, and management indicators.
debtors.committedAmountToleranceTooltip:
Defines what percentage of the agreed amount must be effectively paid for the commitment to be considered fulfilled.
Example: if you set 90%, a commitment of $1,000,000 will be considered fulfilled when the payment reaches at least $900,000.
debtors.daysToleranceTooltip:
Sets how many additional days will be granted after the committed date before marking the commitment as defaulted.
If this period elapses without sufficient payment, the system will automatically classify the commitment as defaulted.
debtors.daysToleranceCriteriaItems: Commitment fulfillment indicator|Debtor credibility measurement|Automatic management prioritization|Activation of alerts or corrective actions
debtors.daysToleranceWarning: A too flexible configuration may hide real risks. A too strict configuration may generate unnecessary alerts and distort portfolio evaluation.

cashFlow.title: Cash flow
cashFlow.description: Complete this section to configure your company's operational data and customize your platform experience.

tooltips.considerLitigation: Defines whether invoices in litigation status will be considered in the cash estimation calculation.
tooltips.litigation: Allows you to define the estimated recovery percentage for documents in litigation.
tooltips.considerCommercialInvoices: Defines whether invoices associated with special commercial agreements will be considered in the estimation.
tooltips.ncCutoffDays: Defines the number of days within which a Credit Note will be considered to adjust the cash estimation.
tooltips.upperRangePercentage: Defines what percentage of credit note amounts exceeding the cutoff days will be incorporated into the estimation, allowing you to control their impact on income projection.
tooltips.lowerRangePercentage: Establishes what percentage of credit notes that do not exceed the cutoff days will impact the cash flow projection.
tooltips.considerAverageMonths: Determines whether the calculation will be based on a historical average of your clients' payment behavior.
tooltips.considerAverageMonthsMore: More months means greater stability in the estimation.
tooltips.considerAverageMonthsLess: Fewer months means greater sensitivity to recent changes.
tooltips.months: Defines the number of historical months to be considered for calculating the average.
tooltips.factor: Allows you to apply a manual adjustment coefficient to the final estimation result.
tooltips.factorMore: A factor greater than 1 increases the projection.
tooltips.factorLess: A factor less than 1 reduces it.
